Name: Venusaur ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ledyba
Flavor-Text: When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.

Name: Ledian
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has 90 HP or less remaining to the Active Spot.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

Name: Celebi
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has the ability to move through time. Records describing it as a forest deity can be found from many different eras.

Name: Lileep
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Lileep once lived in warm seas that existed approximately 100,000,000 years ago.

Name: Cradily
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose Burned, Confused, or Poisoned. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
Effects: This attack does 100 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has short legs and can’t walk very fast, but its neck and tentacles can extend to over three times their usual length to nab distant prey.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Mow Rotom
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools and Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Rotom has possessed a lawn mower. It will mow down grass and flowers, then swagger around with pride at its accomplishments.

Name: Grubbin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.

Name: Gossifleur
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.

Name: Eldegoss
Effects: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck. If you do, search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.

Name: Applin
Flavor-Text: Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

Name: Dipplin
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

Name: Hydrapple ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Nymble
Flavor-Text: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it’s in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

Name: Lokix
Flavor-Text: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

Name: Toedscool
Flavor-Text: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

Name: Toedscruel
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: Its legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Reshiram
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

Name: Salandit
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Only female Salandit can produce gas laden with pheromones. Males entranced by this gas will do whatever the females tell them.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent discards a card from their hand. If this Pokémon evolved from Salandit during this turn, your opponent discards 2 more cards.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When two Salazzle meet, they will use their pheromone gas to fight over the males in each other’s group.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Scorbunny
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny’s footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.

Name: Raboot
Flavor-Text: While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

Name: Cinderace ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flare Strike.
This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Blastoise ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 140 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lapras
Flavor-Text: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

Name: Lapras ex
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Look at the top 20 cards of your deck and attach any number of Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Marill
Flavor-Text: The oil-filled end of its tail floats on water. It keeps Marill from drowning even in a strong current.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of living things on riverbeds.

Name: Finneon
Flavor-Text: It absorbs sunlight with the pink areas of its skin, which then shine. This appears to be a form of camouflage.

Name: Lumineon
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Lumineon swimming in the darkness of the deep sea look like stars shining in the night sky.

Name: Tirtouga
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon inhabited ancient seas. Although it can only crawl, it still comes up onto land in search of prey.

Name: Carracosta
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Evolution Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Carracosta completely devours its prey—bones, shells, and all. Because of this, Carracosta’s own shell grows thick and sturdy.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Frogadier
Flavor-Text: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

Name: Greninja ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crabominable
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost [C] less for each Kofu card in your discard pile.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Haymaker.
Flavor-Text: Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon’s movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: A popular game among children is to lift up sticks that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose Chewtle can hang on the longest.

Name: Drednaw
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon if that damage is 200 or more.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck out and chomps down on them.

Name: Veluza
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost [C] less for each Kofu card in your discard pile.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.

Name: Electivire
Effects: During your next turn, your Pokémon can’t attack. (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent and instantly sends over 20,000 volts of high-voltage electricity through them.

Name: Chinchou
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It shoots positively and negatively charged electricity from the tips of its two antennae and stuns its opponents.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. Put 8 damage counters instead of 3 on that Pokémon for this Special Condition.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.

Name: Joltik
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [G] Energy cards and up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Since it can’t generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

Name: Galvantula ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 110 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Charjabug
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Charjabug and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: As it digests fallen leaves, it generates and stores electricity, which it can discharge from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

Name: Togedemaru
Effects: If you have exactly 1 Prize card remaining, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.

Name: Zeraora
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Slowking
Effects: Discard the top card of your deck, and if that card is a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, choose 1 of its attacks and use it as this attack. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Flavor-Text: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

Name: Mewtwo
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “signpost for wandering spirits.”

Name: Drifblim
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Drifloon and Drifblim in play. This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Drifloon and Drifblim. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They carry people and Pokémon, but the wind can catch them, so there can’t be a fixed destination.

Name: Yamask
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face.

Name: Comfey
Effects: Each player draws 3 cards.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It prefers areas with lots of flowers. A Comfey adorned with flowers is a happy Comfey, and the flowers gain healing effects.

Name: Milcery
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it tackles its foe and covers them in cream. With its foe either blinded or astonished by deliciousness, Milcery flees.

Name: Alcremie
Effects: Search your deck for up to 5 Pokémon that are the same type as any Basic Energy attached to this Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Alcremie that have been cherished produce exquisite cream, so Trainers always raise them lovingly.

Name: Fidough
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.

Name: Dachsbun ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from each of your Evolution Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard all Energy from those Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flittle
Flavor-Text: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

Name: Espathra
Flavor-Text: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

Name: Greavard
Flavor-Text: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

Name: Iron Boulder
Effects: If you don’t have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

Name: Cubone
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.

Name: Marowak
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 40 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
If any of your Benched Cubone have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.

Name: Rhyhorn
Flavor-Text: Once it starts running, it won’t stop—even if it crashes through boulders. This disposition is what’s caused its habitat to expand.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It evolved to walk using only its hind legs, which has allowed it to expand its habitat even onto steep mountains.

Name: Rhyperior
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to all of your Pokémon.
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Rhyperior’s brain is smaller than a Rhydon’s, but the Rhyperior is stronger because it spends more energy on battling and less on brainpower.

Name: Meditite
Flavor-Text: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.

Name: Medicham ex
Effects: Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 50.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions, such as joy and rage, in the form of waves.

Name: Lucario ex
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mienfoo
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: They seclude themselves in the mountains and devote themselves to training. The form of their kicks and chops differs from pack to pack.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: If your opponent has 5 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Using the long fur on its arms like whips, it launches into combo attacks that, once started, no one can stop.

Name: Pancham
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master’s actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.

Name: Diancie
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Special Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

Name: Crabrawler
Flavor-Text: If it loses its pincers, they’ll quickly regrow. The pincers are popular ingredients in paella since their shells produce a tasty soup stock.

Name: Falinks
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
If this Pokémon used Form Ranks during your last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The leader, known as the brass, uses its extendible horn to issue orders to the others when it’s time to change formation.

Name: Garganacl ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach comprises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

Name: Swalot
Effects: If this Pokémon has more Energy attached than your opponent’s Active Pokémon, this attack does 160 more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It can swallow a tire whole in one gulp. It secretes a horribly toxic fluid from the pores on its body.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.

Name: Impidimp
Flavor-Text: They live in groups, pestering and playing pranks on each other to polish their troublemaking skills.

Name: Morgrem
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon absorbs negative emotions and turns them into energy. It’s popular with people who tend to think gloomy thoughts.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.) If you do, this attack does 160 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep within the forest. Even after evolving into this splendid form, it hasn’t given up on its petty misdeeds and pranks.

Name: Bombirdier
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Klink
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

Name: Klang
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon’s rotation speed increases sharply.

Name: Klinklang
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your hand and your opponent has any Stage 2 Pokémon in play, you may put this Pokémon onto your Bench.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

Name: Meltan
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Meltan
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

Name: Melmetal
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from this Pokémon. If you can’t discard any, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.

Name: Melmetal ex
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Duraludon
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its metal body is durable but prone to retaining heat. It vents this heat from the slits in its tail.

Name: Archaludon
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have [M] Energy attached have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.

Name: Varoom
Flavor-Text: The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

Name: Revavroom
Flavor-Text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Orthworm ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Raging Bolt
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.

Name: Tauros
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A rowdy Pokémon with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won’t stop until it hits something.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can evolve into a variety of forms. Eevee’s genes are the key to solving the mysteries of Pokémon evolution.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Since it tells time precisely, some countries consider it to be a deity of wisdom, versed in the ways of the world.

Name: Noctowl
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, if you have any Tera Pokémon in play, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its extremely soft wings make no sound in flight. It silently sneaks up on prey without being detected.

Name: Glameow
Flavor-Text: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

Name: Purugly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It would claim another Pokémon’s nest as its own if it finds a nest sufficiently comfortable.

Name: Fan Rotom
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 [C] Pokémon with 100 HP or less, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Fan Call Ability during your turn.
Effects: If there is no Stadium in play, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: There are several appliances that Rotom can inspirit, but the first one to be developed was the electric fan.

Name: Bouffalant
Ability-Effect: As long as you have at least 1 other Bouffalant in play, all of your Basic [C] Pokémon take 60 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Curly Wall doesn’t stack.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Fletchling can be found in both rural and urban areas. They once had a huge territorial dispute with Squawkabilly. Fights broke out all over town.

Name: Fletchinder
Flavor-Text: Its territory has a radius of just over a mile. If any bird Pokémon should enter, it will show them no mercy.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: If the Retreat Cost of your opponent’s Active Pokémon is [C][C] or more, this attack does 110 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, it vents sparks from the gaps between its feathers. These unburnable feathers are used as a fireproof material.

Name: Wooloo
Effects: You may discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

Name: Dubwool
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: You may discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

Name: Lechonk
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Cyclizar
Flavor-Text: It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider’s body heat warms Cyclizar’s back and lifts the Pokémon’s spirit.

Name: Terapagos ex
Effects: If you go second, you can’t use this attack during your first turn. This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic non-[C] Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Antique Cover Fossil
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Antique Root Fossil
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Basic Pokémon cost [C] more.
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Area Zero Underdepths
Description: Each player who has any Tera Pokémon in play can have up to 8 Pokémon on their Bench.If a player no longer has any Tera Pokémon in play, that player discards Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5. When this card leaves play, both players discard Pokémon from their Bench until they have 5, and the player who played this card discards first.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Briar
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent has exactly 2 Prize cards remaining.During this turn, if your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by your Tera Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Crispin
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards of different types, reveal them, and put 1 of them into your hand. Attach the other to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Deluxe Bomb
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Glass Trumpet
Description: You can use this card only if you have any Tera Pokémon in play.Choose up to 2 of your Benched [C] Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Grand Tree
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Stage 1 Pokémon that evolves from 1 of their Basic Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. If that Pokémon was evolved in this way, that player may search their deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, that player shuffles their deck. (Players can’t evolve a Basic Pokémon during their first turn or a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.)
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Gravity Gemstone
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, the Retreat Cost of both Active Pokémon is [C] more.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Kofu
Description: Put 2 cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck in any order. If you put 2 cards on the bottom of your deck in this way, draw 4 cards. (If you can’t put 2 cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck, you can’t use this card.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Lacey
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Occa Berry
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Payapa Berry
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s [P] Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Sparkling Crystal
Description: When the Tera Pokémon this card is attached to uses an attack, that attack costs 1 Energy less. (The Energy can be of any type.)
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Bulbasaur
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Ledian
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has 90 HP or less remaining to the Active Spot.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

Name: Lileep
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Lileep once lived in warm seas that existed approximately 100,000,000 years ago.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.

Name: Raboot
Flavor-Text: While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

Name: Crabominable
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost [C] less for each Kofu card in your discard pile.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Haymaker.
Flavor-Text: Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon’s movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.

Name: Joltik
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [G] Energy cards and up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Since it can’t generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

Name: Zeraora
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.

Name: Milcery
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it tackles its foe and covers them in cream. With its foe either blinded or astonished by deliciousness, Milcery flees.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

Name: Gulpin
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach comprises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

Name: Archaludon
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have [M] Energy attached have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It’s so durable that being caught in a cave-in won’t faze it.

Name: Hydrapple ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flare Strike.
This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Lapras ex
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Look at the top 20 cards of your deck and attach any number of Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Galvantula ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 110 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Dachsbun ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from each of your Evolution Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, discard all Energy from those Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Medicham ex
Effects: Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 50.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Orthworm ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bravery Charm
Description: The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to gets +50 HP.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.
